nodes.name: PubMed_get_links
nodes.description: Get external links (LinkOut) for a specific PubMed article by its PMID using elink. Returns URLs to full-text providers, supplementary data, related resources, clinical trials, and other external content. Use this to access full-text articles, find supplementary materials, or discover related datasets and resources. Essential for accessing paywalled content via institutional subscriptions or finding open-access versions. Find PMIDs using PubMed_search_articles.

pmid.description: PubMed ID (PMID) for which to retrieve external links (e.g., '19880848', '19822630'). Find PMIDs using PubMed_search_articles.

nodes.name: NCBI_search_nucleotide
nodes.description: Search NCBI Nucleotide database (GenBank/EMBL/RefSeq) by organism, gene name, or keywords. Returns GenBank UIDs which can be used to fetch accession numbers. Supports filtering by organism, gene, strain, sequence type (complete genome, mRNA, RefSeq). Use this to discover DNA/RNA sequences for specific organisms or genes. Returns both EMBL/GenBank accessions (U00096, M15390) and RefSeq accessions (NC_*, NM_*). Follow up with NCBI_fetch_accessions to get actual accession numbers.

organism.description: Organism name to search (e.g., 'Escherichia coli', 'Homo sapiens'). Searches in [Organism] field.

gene.description: Gene name to search (e.g., 'BRCA1', 'TP53'). Searches in [Gene] field.

strain.description: Strain name to filter (e.g., 'K-12', 'MG1655'). Searches in [Strain] field.

keywords.description: Keywords to search in titles (e.g., 'complete genome', 'mitochondrial'). Searches in [Title] field.

seq_type.description: Sequence type filter: 'complete_genome' for complete genomes, 'mrna' for mRNA sequences, 'refseq' for RefSeq curated sequences

query.description: Free-form search query if not using structured filters. Use NCBI query syntax (e.g., '(Escherichia coli[Organism]) AND (K-12[Strain])')

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 20, max: 100)

nodes.name: NCBI_fetch_accessions
nodes.description: Convert GenBank UIDs to accession numbers (U00096, NC_045512, etc.). Takes UIDs from NCBI_search_nucleotide and returns actual accession numbers that can be used with database tools. Returns both EMBL/GenBank accessions (U*, M*, AJ*) that work with ENA Browser tools, and RefSeq accessions (NC_*, NM_*) for NCBI tools. Use this as second step after NCBI_search_nucleotide to get usable accession numbers.

uids.description: GenBank UID(s) from NCBI_search_nucleotide. Can be single UID or array of UIDs.

nodes.name: NCBI_get_sequence
nodes.description: Retrieve DNA/RNA sequence data from NCBI by accession number. Returns sequences in specified format (fasta, gb, embl). Works with both EMBL/GenBank accessions (U00096, M15390) and RefSeq accessions (NC_045512, NM_000546). Use this to get actual sequence data after finding accessions with NCBI_search_nucleotide. Returns full sequence with annotations depending on format.

accession.description: NCBI accession number (e.g., 'U00096', 'NC_045512', 'NM_000546'). Works with GenBank, EMBL, and RefSeq accessions.

format.description: Sequence format: 'fasta' for FASTA, 'gb' for GenBank, 'embl' for EMBL, 'gp' for GenPept protein. Default: fasta

nodes.name: NCBI_SRA_search_runs
nodes.description: Search NCBI Sequence Read Archive (SRA) for NGS/RNA-seq sequencing runs by study accession, organism, sequencing strategy (RNA-Seq, WGS, ChIP-Seq), platform (ILLUMINA, OXFORD_NANOPORE), or library source. Returns SRA UIDs which can be used with other SRA tools to retrieve metadata and download links. Essential for discovering high-throughput sequencing data from public repositories. Use this to find relevant sequencing experiments before accessing the actual FASTQ data files.

study.description: Study accession to search (e.g., 'SRP000001', 'ERP000001', 'DRP000001'). Searches in [Study] field.

organism.description: Organism name to filter (e.g., 'Homo sapiens', 'Mus musculus', 'SARS-CoV-2'). Searches in [Organism] field.

strategy.description: Sequencing strategy to filter. Common values: 'RNA-Seq' (transcriptomics), 'WGS' (whole genome), 'WXS' (exome), 'ChIP-Seq' (chromatin), 'ATAC-Seq' (accessibility), 'Bisulfite-Seq' (methylation), 'AMPLICON' (targeted), 'Hi-C' (3D genome)

platform.description: Sequencing platform to filter. Common values: 'ILLUMINA', 'OXFORD_NANOPORE', 'PACBIO_SMRT', 'ION_TORRENT', 'BGISEQ'

source.description: Library source to filter. Common values: 'GENOMIC' (DNA), 'TRANSCRIPTOMIC' (RNA), 'METAGENOMIC', 'METATRANSCRIPTOMIC', 'SYNTHETIC', 'VIRAL_RNA'

query.description: Free-form search query if not using structured filters. Use NCBI query syntax (e.g., '(Homo sapiens[Organism]) AND (RNA-Seq[Strategy])')

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 20, max: 100)

nodes.name: NCBI_SRA_get_run_info
nodes.description: Get detailed metadata for SRA run accessions including sequencing platform (ILLUMINA, Oxford Nanopore), instrument model, library strategy (RNA-Seq, WGS), library source (genomic, transcriptomic), library layout (paired/single), sample information, study details, organism, and data statistics (total spots, bases). Essential for understanding experimental design and data characteristics before downloading large FASTQ files. Use SRA run accessions (SRR000001, ERR000001, DRR000001) from NCBI_SRA_search_runs or known accessions.

accessions.description: SRA run accession(s) (e.g., 'SRR000001', 'ERR000001', 'DRR000001'). Can be single accession or array of accessions. Use SRR for NCBI, ERR for ENA, DRR for DDBJ.

nodes.name: NCBI_SRA_get_download_urls
nodes.description: Get FTP and cloud storage URLs for downloading FASTQ sequencing data from SRA runs. Returns multiple access methods: NCBI FTP URLs for direct .sra file download, AWS S3 URLs for cloud-based access (faster for large-scale analysis), and NCBI web URLs for browser access. Note: Downloaded .sra files require SRA Toolkit (fastq-dump or fasterq-dump) to convert to standard FASTQ format. Essential for programmatic access to petabytes of public sequencing data. Works with SRR (NCBI), ERR (ENA), and DRR (DDBJ) accessions.

accessions.description: SRA run accession(s) (e.g., 'SRR000001', 'ERR000001', 'DRR000001'). Can be single accession or array of accessions.

nodes.name: NCBI_SRA_link_to_biosample
nodes.description: Link SRA sequencing runs to their associated BioSample records to access biological sample metadata including sample attributes, tissue type, cell line, treatment conditions, donor information, and collection details. BioSample provides rich contextual information about the biological specimens used in sequencing experiments. Essential for understanding experimental context and sample provenance. Returns BioSample UIDs which can be used with NCBI BioSample API to retrieve detailed sample metadata. Works with SRA UIDs from NCBI_SRA_search_runs.

accessions.description: SRA UID(s) from NCBI_SRA_search_runs. Can be single UID or array of UIDs. These are NCBI internal IDs (numeric), not SRR accessions.

nodes.name: DOAJ_search_articles
nodes.description: Search DOAJ (Directory of Open Access Journals) for open-access articles. Returns articles with title, authors, year, DOI, venue, and URL.

query.description: Search query for DOAJ articles. Supports Lucene syntax for advanced queries.

max_results.description: Maximum number of articles to return. Default is 10, maximum is 100.

type.description: Type of search: 'articles' or 'journals'. Default is 'articles'.

nodes.name: Unpaywall_check_oa_status
nodes.description: Query Unpaywall by DOI to check open-access status and OA locations. Requires a contact email for API access.

doi.description: DOI (Digital Object Identifier) of the article to check for open access status.

email.description: Contact email address required by Unpaywall API for polite usage tracking.

nodes.name: BioRxiv_get_preprint
nodes.description: Get full metadata for a specific bioRxiv or medRxiv preprint by its DOI. Returns comprehensive metadata including title, authors, abstract, publication date, version, category, corresponding author, funding information, and URLs (HTML, PDF, XML). Response time: <1 second. Use when you have a bioRxiv DOI (format: 10.1101/YYYY.MM.DD.XXXXXX). To SEARCH for preprints by keywords, use EuropePMC_search_articles with 'SRC:PPR' filter (fast, relevance-ranked), or web_search with 'site:biorxiv.org'. bioRxiv API does not support text search.

doi.description: bioRxiv or medRxiv DOI. Can be full DOI (e.g., '10.1101/2023.12.01.569554') or just the numeric part after '10.1101/' (e.g., '2023.12.01.569554'). Find DOIs using EuropePMC_search_articles, web_search, or from paper citations.

server.description: Server to query: 'biorxiv' for bioRxiv preprints or 'medrxiv' for medRxiv preprints. Default is 'biorxiv'.

nodes.name: MedRxiv_get_preprint
nodes.description: Get full metadata for a specific medRxiv preprint by its DOI. Returns comprehensive metadata including title, authors, abstract, publication date, version, category, and URLs. Response time: <1 second. Use when you have a medRxiv DOI (format: 10.1101/YYYY.MM.DD.XXXXXXX). To SEARCH for medRxiv preprints by keywords, use EuropePMC_search_articles with 'SRC:PPR' filter (fast, relevance-ranked), or web_search with 'site:medrxiv.org'. medRxiv API does not support text search.

doi.description: medRxiv DOI. Can be full DOI (e.g., '10.1101/2021.04.29.21256344') or just the numeric part after '10.1101/' (e.g., '2021.04.29.21256344'). Find DOIs using EuropePMC_search_articles, web_search, or from paper citations.

server.description: Server to query - always 'medrxiv' for this tool.

nodes.name: HAL_search_archive
nodes.description: Search the French HAL open archive via its public API. Returns documents with title, authors, year, DOI, URL, abstract, and source.

query.description: Search query for HAL archive. Supports Lucene syntax for advanced queries.

max_results.description: Maximum number of documents to return. Default is 10, maximum is 100.

nodes.name: CORE_search_papers
nodes.description: Search for open access academic papers using CORE API. CORE aggregates OA repository and journal content; queries can match metadata and (for many records) indexed full text, but coverage varies by source. Use CORE_get_fulltext_snippets to search inside a returned PDF URL when exact body-text terms matter.

query.description: Search query for CORE papers. Use keywords separated by spaces to refine your search.

q.description: Alias for `query`.

limit.description: Maximum number of papers to return. This sets the maximum number of papers retrieved from CORE.

page_size.description: Alias for `limit`.

max_results.description: Alias for `limit`.

year_from.description: Start year for publication date filter (e.g., 2020). Optional parameter to limit search to papers published from this year onwards.

year_to.description: End year for publication date filter (e.g., 2024). Optional parameter to limit search to papers published up to this year.

language.description: Language filter for papers (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr'). Optional parameter to limit search to papers in specific language.

nodes.name: CORE_get_fulltext_snippets
nodes.description: Fetch an open-access PDF (commonly returned by CORE_search_papers) and return bounded text snippets around provided terms. This is a local (download + scan) full-text search fallback when index-based search misses body-only keywords. Extraction backends: PyMuPDF (fitz), pypdf, or markitdown (auto-selects fastest available unless `extractor` is set).

pdf_url.description: Direct PDF URL to download (preferred). CORE_search_papers returns this as the `url` field in many results.

url.description: Alias for `pdf_url` for convenience when piping CORE_search_papers outputs.

terms.description: Terms to search for in the extracted PDF text (case-insensitive).

window_chars.description: Context window size (characters) before and after each match.

max_snippets_per_term.description: Maximum number of snippets returned per term.

max_total_chars.description: Hard cap on total characters returned across all snippets.

extractor.description: PDF text extraction backend: auto (default), fitz (PyMuPDF), pypdf, or markitdown.

timeout.description: Download timeout in seconds (bounded to <=55 to stay under typical MCP call deadlines).

max_pdf_bytes.description: Maximum PDF size (bytes) allowed for download/scan. Prevents timeouts on huge PDFs.

max_pages.description: Maximum number of PDF pages to scan when using fitz/pypdf extractors.

max_text_chars.description: Maximum number of extracted text characters to scan for term matches (limits CPU/memory).

nodes.name: PMC_search_papers
nodes.description: Search for full-text biomedical literature using the PMC (PubMed Central) API. PMC is the free full-text archive of biomedical and life sciences journal literature at the U.S. National Institutes of Health's National Library of Medicine. Uses `limit` (default 10); accepts `retmax` as an alias for `limit`.

query.description: Search query for PMC papers. Use keywords separated by spaces to refine your search.

limit.description: Maximum number of papers to return. This sets the maximum number of papers retrieved from PMC.

retmax.description: Alias for limit (NCBI eutils naming). If both retmax and limit are provided, limit is used.

date_from.description: Start date for publication date filter (YYYY/MM/DD format). Optional parameter to limit search to papers published from this date onwards.

date_to.description: End date for publication date filter (YYYY/MM/DD format). Optional parameter to limit search to papers published up to this date.

article_type.description: Article type filter (e.g., 'research-article', 'review', 'case-report'). Optional parameter to limit search to specific article types.

include_abstract.description: If true, attempts to enrich results with an abstract (best-effort) by fetching PubMed abstracts for items that have a PMID.

nodes.name: Zenodo_search_records
nodes.description: Search Zenodo for research data, publications, and datasets. Zenodo is an open-access repository that hosts research outputs from all fields of science, including papers, datasets, software, and more. Returns records with title, authors, DOI, and file information. Use this to discover datasets for your research topic, find citable DOIs, or explore community repositories. Find record IDs to use with Zenodo_get_record for complete metadata.

query.description: Free text search query for Zenodo records. Use keywords to search across titles, descriptions, authors, and other metadata.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must be between 1 and 200.

community.description: Optional community slug to filter results by specific research community (e.g., 'zenodo', 'ecfunded').

nodes.name: Zenodo_get_record
nodes.description: Get detailed metadata for a specific Zenodo record by its record ID. Returns comprehensive information including title, description, authors, DOI, publication date, keywords, license, file information, citations, and related identifiers. Use this to get complete metadata for a record found via search or when you have a specific Zenodo record ID or DOI. Find record IDs from Zenodo_search_records (format: integer like 1234567).

record_id.description: Zenodo record identifier (e.g., 1234567, 7654321). Find record IDs using Zenodo_search_records or from publication DOIs (DOI format: 10.5281/zenodo.1234567).

nodes.name: Zenodo_get_record_files
nodes.description: Get the list of files for a specific Zenodo record including file names, sizes, checksums, and download URLs. Returns detailed file metadata essential for downloading datasets, code, or supplementary materials. Each file includes direct download URL, MD5 checksum for verification, file type, and size in bytes. Use this to access the actual data files from a Zenodo record. Find record IDs from Zenodo_search_records or Zenodo_get_record.

record_id.description: Zenodo record identifier (e.g., 1234567). Find record IDs using Zenodo_search_records.

nodes.name: Zenodo_list_licenses
nodes.description: List available licenses that can be applied to Zenodo uploads. Returns license metadata including license ID, title, URL, and description. Use this to discover available open licenses (e.g., Creative Commons, MIT, GPL) for your data uploads, or to understand the licensing terms of existing records. Essential for researchers planning to upload data or checking licensing compliance.

query.description: Optional search query to filter licenses by name or keyword (e.g., 'creative commons', 'MIT', 'GPL').

limit.description: Maximum number of licenses to return (default: 25, max: 100).

nodes.name: Zenodo_get_license
nodes.description: Get detailed information about a specific license by its license ID. Returns complete license metadata including title, URL, description, domains of applicability (content/data/software), and license status. Use this to understand the specific terms and conditions of a license before applying it to your uploads or when checking the licensing of existing records. Find license IDs using Zenodo_list_licenses (common IDs: 'cc-by-4.0', 'cc-zero', 'MIT', 'Apache-2.0', 'GPL-3.0').

license_id.description: License identifier (e.g., 'cc-by-4.0', 'MIT', 'Apache-2.0', 'GPL-3.0'). Find license IDs using Zenodo_list_licenses. Common IDs include Creative Commons licenses (cc-by, cc-by-sa, cc-zero), open source licenses (MIT, Apache, GPL), and others.

nodes.name: OpenAIRE_search_publications
nodes.description: Search OpenAIRE Explore for research products including publications, datasets, and software. OpenAIRE is the European open science platform that provides access to research outputs from EU-funded projects.

query.description: Search query for OpenAIRE research products. Use keywords to search across titles, abstracts, and metadata.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return. Default is 10, maximum is 100.

type.description: Type of research product to search: 'publications', 'datasets', or 'software'. Default is 'publications'.

nodes.name: OSF_search_preprints
nodes.description: Search OSF (Open Science Framework) Preprints for research preprints and working papers. OSF Preprints aggregates preprints from multiple providers including OSF, PsyArXiv, and others.

query.description: Search query for OSF preprints. Use keywords to search across titles and abstracts.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return. Default is 10, maximum is 100.

provider.description: Optional preprint provider filter (e.g., 'osf', 'psyarxiv', 'socarxiv'). If not specified, searches all providers.

nodes.name: Fatcat_search_scholar
nodes.description: Search Internet Archive Scholar via Fatcat releases search and retrieve detailed metadata including authors, publication year, DOI, journal, publisher, abstract, and PDF links. Fatcat is the underlying database powering Internet Archive Scholar, providing access to 140+ million research papers and academic publications.

query.description: Search query for Fatcat releases. Use keywords to search across titles, abstracts, and metadata of research papers.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return. Default is 10, maximum is 100.

nodes.name: Wikidata_SPARQL_query
nodes.description: Execute SPARQL queries against Wikidata to retrieve structured data. This tool powers Scholia-style visualizations and can query academic topics, authors, institutions, and research relationships.

sparql.description: SPARQL query string to execute against Wikidata. Use SPARQL syntax to query academic entities, relationships, and properties.

max_results.description: Optional result limit override. If not specified, uses the LIMIT clause in the SPARQL query or returns all results.

nodes.name: Wikipedia_search
nodes.description: Search Wikipedia articles using MediaWiki API. Returns a list of matching articles with titles, snippets, and metadata. No API key required.

query.description: Search query string to find Wikipedia articles

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10, max: 50)

language.description: Wikipedia language code (e.g., 'en' for English, 'zh' for Chinese, 'fr' for French). Default: 'en'

nodes.name: Wikipedia_get_content
nodes.description: Extract content from a Wikipedia article. Can extract introduction, summary, or full article content. No API key required.

title.description: Wikipedia article title (exact title as it appears on Wikipedia)

language.description: Wikipedia language code (e.g., 'en' for English, 'zh' for Chinese). Default: 'en'

extract_type.description: Type of content to extract: 'intro' (first paragraph only), 'summary' (first few paragraphs, default), or 'full' (entire article)

max_chars.description: Maximum characters to return for intro/summary (ignored for 'full'). Default: 2000

nodes.name: Wikipedia_get_summary
nodes.description: Get a brief summary/introduction from a Wikipedia article. This is a convenience tool that extracts just the first paragraph(s) of an article. No API key required.

title.description: Wikipedia article title (exact title as it appears on Wikipedia)

language.description: Wikipedia language code (e.g., 'en' for English). Default: 'en'

nodes.name: DBpedia_SPARQL_query
nodes.description: Execute SPARQL queries against DBpedia to retrieve structured knowledge. DBpedia extracts structured information from Wikipedia and makes it available as linked data. This tool is particularly useful for querying information about people, places, organizations, works, and other entities, especially in humanities and social sciences domains. No API key required.

sparql.description: SPARQL query string to execute against DBpedia. Use SPARQL syntax to query entities, relationships, and properties. DBpedia uses different property names than Wikidata (e.g., dbo:abstract, dbo:birthDate, rdfs:label).

max_results.description: Optional result limit override. If not specified, uses the LIMIT clause in the SPARQL query or returns all results.

nodes.name: ScientificTextSummarizer
nodes.description: Summarizes biomedical research texts, abstracts, or papers with specified length and focus areas. Uses AI to extract key findings, methodology, and conclusions from complex biomedical literature.

text.description: The biomedical text, abstract, or paper content to be summarized.

focus_area.description: What to focus on in the summary (e.g., 'methodology', 'results', 'clinical implications', 'drug interactions').

nodes.name: MedicalLiteratureReviewer
nodes.description: Conducts systematic reviews of medical literature on specific topics. Synthesizes findings from multiple studies and provides evidence-based conclusions with structured analysis and quality assessment.

research_topic.description: The specific medical/research topic for literature review (e.g., 'efficacy of drug X in treating condition Y').

literature_content.description: The literature content, abstracts, full studies, or research papers to review and synthesize.

focus_area.description: Primary focus area for the review (e.g., 'therapeutic efficacy', 'safety profile', 'diagnostic accuracy', 'biomarker validation').

study_types.description: Types of studies to prioritize in the analysis (e.g., 'randomized controlled trials', 'meta-analyses', 'cohort studies', 'case-control studies').

quality_level.description: Minimum evidence quality level to include (e.g., 'high quality only', 'moderate and above', 'all available evidence').

review_scope.description: Scope of the review (e.g., 'comprehensive systematic review', 'rapid review', 'scoping review', 'narrative review').

nodes.name: HypothesisGenerator
nodes.description: Generates research hypotheses based on provided background context, domain, and desired format. Uses AI to propose novel, testable hypotheses for scientific exploration.

context.description: Background information, observations, or data description from which to derive hypotheses.

domain.description: Field of study or research area (e.g., 'neuroscience', 'ecology', 'materials science').

hypothesis_format.description:
Optional directive on how to structure each hypothesis. Choose from one of the following formats:

1. If–Then Statements: "If [independent variable condition], then [expected outcome]."
2. Null and Alternative (Statistical):
   • H₀ (Null): "There is no difference/effect/association between X and Y."
   • H₁ (Alt): "There is a difference/effect/association between X and Y."
3. Associative (Correlation-Focused): "There is a relationship/association between [Variable A] and [Variable B]."
4. Directional (Non-If–Then): "Increasing/decreasing [Variable A] will lead to [directional change] in [Variable B]."
5. Comparative (Group Comparison): "Group A will show higher/lower [dependent measure] compared to Group B under [condition]."
6. Mechanistic: "Because [mechanism or process], [Variable A] will cause [Variable B]."
7. Descriptive (Exploratory/Pattern-Oriented): "Population X exhibits pattern Y in context Z."

If omitted, defaults to concise declarative sentences.

nodes.name: ExperimentalDesignScorer
nodes.description: Assesses a proposed experimental design by assigning scores and structured feedback on hypothesis clarity, variable definitions, sample size, controls, randomization, measurement methods, statistical analysis, bias mitigation, ethical considerations, and overall feasibility.

hypothesis.description: A clear statement of the research hypothesis to be tested.

design_description.description: A detailed description of the proposed experimental design, including variables, methods, sample details, and planned analyses.

nodes.name: MedicalTermNormalizer
nodes.description: Identifies and corrects misspelled drug or disease names, returning a list of plausible standardized terms.

raw_terms.description: A comma- or whitespace-separated string containing one misspelled drug or disease name.

nodes.name: NoveltySignificanceReviewer
nodes.description: Provides a structured peer-review of the work's originality and potential impact.

nodes.name: LiteratureContextReviewer
nodes.description: Reviews coverage, relevance, and critical synthesis of prior scholarship.

nodes.name: MethodologyRigorReviewer
nodes.description: Evaluates design appropriateness, sampling, and procedural transparency.

nodes.name: DataAnalysisValidityReviewer
nodes.description: Checks statistical choices, assumption testing, and reporting transparency.

nodes.name: ResultsInterpretationReviewer
nodes.description: Judges whether conclusions are data-justified and limitations addressed.

nodes.name: WritingPresentationReviewer
nodes.description: Assesses clarity, organization, grammar, and visual presentation quality.

nodes.name: ReproducibilityTransparencyReviewer
nodes.description: Evaluates data, code, and protocol availability for replication.

nodes.name: EthicalComplianceReviewer
nodes.description: Checks adherence to ethical standards and disclosure practices.

nodes.name: QuestionRephraser
nodes.description: Generates three distinct paraphrases of a given question while ensuring answer options remain valid and applicable.

question.description: The original question text to be rephrased

options.description: Answer options (e.g., multiple choice options) that should remain valid for the rephrased questions. Leave empty if no options are provided.

nodes.name: ProtocolOptimizer
nodes.description: Reviews an initial protocol and delivers targeted revisions that improve clarity, feasibility, risk-management, and evaluation rigor.

nodes.name: AdvancedCodeQualityAnalyzer
nodes.description: Performs deep analysis of code quality including complexity, security, performance, and maintainability metrics with domain-specific expertise

source_code.description: The source code to analyze for quality assessment

language.description: Programming language (python, javascript, etc.)

analysis_depth.description: Level of analysis depth to perform

domain_context.description: Domain context for specialized analysis (e.g., bioinformatics, web development)

nodes.name: DomainExpertValidator
nodes.description: Provides domain-specific validation and expert recommendations for tools with deep expertise across scientific and technical domains

tool_config.description: JSON string of tool configuration to validate

validation_aspects.description: JSON array string of specific aspects to validate

nodes.name: ToolQualityEvaluator
nodes.description: Evaluates the quality of tool configurations and implementations. Provides detailed scoring and feedback for improvement.

tool_config.description: JSON string of the tool configuration

evaluation_aspects.description: Aspects to evaluate (functionality, usability, completeness, best_practices)

nodes.name: LabelGenerator
nodes.description: Generates relevant keyword labels for tools based on their name, description, parameters, and category. Creates a comprehensive list of tags for tool discovery and categorization.

tool_description.description: Detailed description of what the tool does

tool_parameters.description: JSON string describing the tool's input parameters and their types

category.description: The general category or domain the tool belongs to

existing_labels.description: JSON array string of existing labels to consider reusing (optional)

nodes.name: call_agentic_human
nodes.description: Produces a concise, practical answer that emulates how a well-informed human would respond to the question.

question.description: The user's question to be answered in a human-like manner.

nodes.name: ToolMetadataGenerator
nodes.description: Generates a JSON structure with the metadata of a tool in ToolUniverse, given the JSON configuration of the tool.

tool_config.description: JSON string of the tool configuration to extract metadata from

tool_type_mappings.description: A mapping from a simplified toolType to a list of tool_config.type that fall under the toolType (e.g., {'Databases': ['XMLTool']})

nodes.name: ToolMetadataStandardizer
nodes.description: Standardizes and groups semantically equivalent metadata strings (e.g., sources, tags) into canonical forms for consistent downstream usage.

metadata_list.description: List of raw metadata strings (e.g., sources, tags) to standardize and group.

limit.description: If provided, the maximum number of canonical strings to return. The LLM will group terms more aggressively to meet this limit, ensuring all raw strings are mapped.

nodes.name: ToolRelationshipDetector
nodes.description: Analyzes a primary tool against a list of other tools to identify meaningful, directional data flow compatibilities for scientific workflows. Returns a list of compatible pairs with direction and rationale.

tool_a.description: JSON string for the primary tool configuration (Tool A).

other_tools.description: JSON string of a list of other tool configurations to compare against Tool A.

nodes.name: advanced_literature_search_agent
nodes.description: Advanced multi-agent deep literature search system. This is a SEARCH-FIRST system: agents must extensively use ToolUniverse literature search tools to gather information, NOT rely on their own knowledge. Required pipeline: (1) query_planner analyzes the query to identify key search aspects and generates 3-8 focused search queries; (2) FIRST call web_foundation_searcher (base web tools only) to produce seeds/sources/keywords; (3) For EACH seed query, parallel call ALL THREE specialized searchers: medical_literature_searcher (PubMed, EuropePMC, PMC, MedRxiv, BioRxiv), computer_science_searcher (ArXiv, DBLP, SemanticScholar), and general_literature_searcher (openalex, Crossref, DOAJ, CORE) using concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor; (4) result_analyzer deduplicates and scores results from all searchers; (5) literature_synthesizer generates the final report strictly from searched results. CRITICAL: All information must come from tool searches, not pre-existing knowledge.

query.description: Research query or topic to search in academic literature. The agent will automatically determine search strategy, database selection, filters, and result limits based on the query content and research domain.

nodes.name: open_deep_research_agent
nodes.description: Research manager agent that decomposes the user task, delegates focused subtasks to domain sub‑agents (web researcher, synthesizer), enforces evidence use, requires numeric outputs with units, and returns a concise final answer with citations. It should: (1) draft a brief plan, (2) ask web_researcher to gather authoritative facts (URLs + extracted numbers), (3) validate consistency across sources, (4) instruct synthesizer to compute/compose the final result, and (5) output only the final, unit‑aware answer plus one short rationale line.

nodes.name: UnifiedToolGenerator
nodes.description: Generates complete ToolUniverse tools using simplified XML format that simultaneously creates both implementation code and specification

tool_description.description: Description of the desired tool functionality

reference_info.description: JSON string containing curated reference information including API documentation and package recommendations

xml_template.description: XML template example showing the expected format with code and spec sections

nodes.name: XMLToolOptimizer
nodes.description: Optimizes tools defined in XML format based on test results and quality feedback

xml_tool.description: Current XML-formatted tool definition with code and spec sections

optimization_context.description: JSON string containing test results, quality feedback, iteration info, improvement history, and any special instructions

nodes.name: ReferenceInfoAnalyzer
nodes.description: Analyzes and curates reference information to provide high-quality context for tool generation

tool_description.description: Description of the tool being generated

raw_reference_info.description: JSON string containing raw reference information from web search and package discovery

nodes.name: PackageAnalyzer
nodes.description: Analyzes package candidates and recommends the best options based on quality metrics and suitability

tool_description.description: Description of the tool being generated

package_candidates.description: JSON string containing package candidates with their metadata (from PyPI, search results, etc.)

nodes.name: TestResultsAnalyzer
nodes.description: Analyzes test execution results and extracts key issues for targeted optimization

test_results.description: JSON string containing test execution results with pass/fail status, error messages, and tracebacks

tool_implementation.description: Current tool implementation code for context

nodes.name: CodeQualityAnalyzer
nodes.description: Analyzes code quality from multiple dimensions including algorithmic correctness, functional implementation capability, performance characteristics, and best practices. Provides detailed feedback and improvement suggestions.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool being analyzed

tool_description.description: Description of what the tool is supposed to do

tool_parameters.description: JSON string of tool parameters and their types

implementation_code.description: The actual implementation code to analyze

test_cases.description: JSON string of test cases for the tool

test_execution_results.description: JSON string of test execution results including pass/fail status and actual outputs

nodes.name: ToolDiscover
nodes.description: Generates new ToolUniverse-compliant tools based on short descriptions using XML format for simultaneous code and specification generation. Automatically discovers similar tools, curates high-quality reference information, and iteratively optimizes the tool using agentic optimization.

tool_description.description: Short description of the desired tool functionality

save_to_file.description: Whether to save the generated tool files

output_file.description: Optional file path to save the generated tool

save_dir.description: Directory path to save the generated tool files (defaults to current working directory)

nodes.name: web_search
nodes.description: General web search using DDGS (Dux Distributed Global Search) supporting multiple search engines including Google, Bing, Brave, Yahoo, and DuckDuckGo. No API keys required.

query.description: Search query string

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

nodes.name: web_api_documentation_search
nodes.description: Specialized web search for API documentation, Python packages, and technical resources using DDGS with multiple search engines. Optimized for finding official documentation and library information.

query.description: Search query string (e.g., tool name, library name, API name)

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

nodes.name: dynamic_package_discovery
nodes.description: Dynamically searches PyPI and evaluates packages based on requirements

requirements.description: Description of what the package should do

nodes.name: PyPIPackageInspector
nodes.description: Extracts comprehensive package information from PyPI and GitHub for quality evaluation. Provides detailed metrics on popularity (downloads, stars, forks), maintenance (release frequency, recent activity), documentation quality, Python version compatibility, and security indicators. Returns an overall quality score (0-100) with breakdown by category.

package_name.description: Name of the Python package to inspect on PyPI (e.g., 'requests', 'numpy', 'pandas')

include_github.description: Whether to fetch GitHub repository statistics (stars, forks, issues, last push). Requires package to have GitHub URL in metadata.

include_downloads.description: Whether to fetch download statistics from pypistats.org (downloads per day/week/month)

nodes.name: DiseaseAnalyzerAgent
nodes.description: AI agent that analyzes disease characteristics and identifies potential therapeutic targets using LLM reasoning

disease_name.description: Name of the disease to analyze

context.description: Additional context or specific focus areas

nodes.name: CompoundDiscoveryAgent
nodes.description: AI agent that analyzes potential drug compounds using multiple strategies and LLM reasoning

nodes.name: ADMETAnalyzerAgent
nodes.description: AI agent that analyzes ADMET data and provides insights on drug-likeness and safety profiles

admet_data.description: ADMET data from computational tools to analyze

nodes.name: DrugInteractionAnalyzerAgent
nodes.description: AI agent that analyzes drug-drug interactions and provides clinical recommendations

compounds.description: List of compounds to analyze for interactions (comma-separated)

nodes.name: LiteratureSynthesisAgent
nodes.description: AI agent that synthesizes literature findings and provides evidence-based insights

literature_data.description: Literature findings or abstracts to synthesize

nodes.name: DrugOptimizationAgent
nodes.description: AI agent that analyzes drug optimization strategies based on ADMET and efficacy data

nodes.name: ClinicalTrialDesignAgent
nodes.description: AI agent that designs clinical trial protocols based on preclinical data and regulatory requirements

indication.description: Disease indication

nodes.name: drugbank_vocab_search
nodes.description: Search the DrugBank vocabulary dataset for drugs by name, ID, synonyms, or other fields using text-based queries. Returns detailed drug information including DrugBank ID, common name, CAS number, UNII, and synonyms.

query.description: Search query string. Can be drug name, synonym, DrugBank ID, or any text to search for.

search_fields.description: Fields to search in. Available fields: 'DrugBank ID', 'Accession Numbers', 'Common name', 'CAS', 'UNII', 'Synonyms', 'Standard InChI Key'.

case_sensitive.description: Whether the search should be case sensitive.

exact_match.description: Whether to perform exact matching instead of substring matching.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

nodes.name: drugbank_vocab_filter
nodes.description: Filter the DrugBank vocabulary dataset based on specific field criteria. Use simple field-value pairs to filter drugs by properties like names, IDs, and chemical identifiers.

condition.description: The type of filtering condition to apply. Filter is case-insensitive.

value.description: The value to filter by. Not required when condition is 'not_empty'. Examples: 'insulin' (for contains), 'DB00' (for starts_with), 'acid' (for ends_with), 'Aspirin' (for exact)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

nodes.name: drugbank_full_search
nodes.description: Search the cleaned DrugBank dataframe (one row per drug) by ID, common name, or synonym. Returns identifiers, ATC, main pharmacology text fields, and protein partners. For best results, it is recommended that one uses `drugbank_vocab_search` to obtain DrugBank ID from other keywords first, and use this tool with DrugBank ID.

query.description: Free-text query (e.g. 'DB00945', 'acetylsalicylic', 'Acarbosa').

search_fields.description: Columns to search in. Choose from: 'drugbank_id', 'name', 'synonyms'.

case_sensitive.description: Match text with exact case if true.

exact_match.description: Field value must equal query exactly if true; otherwise substring match.

limit.description: Max number of rows to return.

nodes.name: drugbank_links_search
nodes.description: Search the cross-reference table linking DrugBank IDs to external identifiers (CAS, KEGG, PubChem, ChEBI, PharmGKB, UniProt, etc.) and web resources.

query.description: Free-text query (e.g. 'DB00002', 'Cetuximab').

search_fields.description: Columns to search. Choose from: 'DrugBank ID', 'Name', 'CAS Number', 'Drug Type', 'KEGG Compound ID', 'KEGG Drug ID', 'PubChem Compound ID', 'PubChem Substance ID', 'ChEBI ID', 'PharmGKB ID', 'HET ID', 'UniProt ID', 'Wikipedia ID', 'Drugs.com Link', 'NDC ID', 'ChemSpider ID', 'BindingDB ID', 'TTD ID'.

case_sensitive.description: Match text with exact case if true.

exact_match.description: Field value must equal query exactly if true; otherwise substring match.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

nodes.name: dict_search
nodes.description: Search the DICTrank dataset for drug-induced cardiotoxicity (DICT) risk information by trade name, generic name, or active ingredient. Searching with exact match is not recommeded.

query.description: Free-text query (e.g. 'ZYPREXA', 'Olanzapine').

search_fields.description: Columns to search. Choose from: 'Trade Name', 'Generic/Proper Name(s)', 'Active Ingredient(s)'.

case_sensitive.description: Match text with exact case if true.

exact_match.description: Field value must equal query exactly if true; otherwise substring match.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

nodes.name: dili_search
nodes.description: Search the DILIrank dataset for drug-induced liver-injury (DILI) risk information by compound name. Searching with exact match is not recommeded.

query.description: Free-text query (e.g. 'acetaminophen').

search_fields.description: Columns to search. Choose from: 'Compound Name'.

case_sensitive.description: Match text with exact case if true.

exact_match.description: Field value must equal query exactly if true; otherwise substring match.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

nodes.name: diqt_search
nodes.description: Search the DIQTA dataset for drug-induced QT-interval prolongation risk information by generic name or DrugBank ID. Searching with exact match is not recommeded for generic name.

query.description: Free-text query (e.g. 'Astemizole', 'DB00637').

search_fields.description: Columns to search. Choose from: 'Generic/Proper Name(s)', 'DrugBank ID'.

case_sensitive.description: Match text with exact case if true.

exact_match.description: Field value must equal query exactly if true; otherwise substring match.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

nodes.name: AdverseEventPredictionQuestionGenerator
nodes.description: Generates a set of personalized adverse‐event prediction questions for a given disease and drug, across multiple patient subgroups.

disease_name.description: The name of the disease or condition

nodes.name: AdverseEventICDMapper
nodes.description: Extracts adverse events from narrative clinical or pharmacovigilance text and maps each event to the most specific ICD-10-CM code.

source_text.description: Unstructured narrative text that may mention adverse events.

nodes.name: AdverseEventPredictionQuestionGeneratorWithContext
nodes.description: Generates a set of personalized adverse‐event prediction questions for a given disease and drug, incorporating additional context information such as patient medical history, clinical findings, or research data.

disease_name.description: The name of the disease or condition

context_information.description: Additional context information such as patient medical history, clinical findings, research data, or other relevant background information that should inform the adverse event prediction questions

nodes.name: DailyMed_search_spls
nodes.description: Search SPL list using multiple filter conditions (drug_name/ndc/rxcui/setid) and return metadata + data array.

drug_name.description: Generic or brand name of the drug, e.g., 'TAMSULOSIN HYDROCHLORIDE'.

setid.description: Set ID corresponding to the SPL.

pagesize.description: Number of items per page, maximum 100, default 100.

nodes.name: DailyMed_get_spl_by_setid
nodes.description: Get complete label corresponding to SPL Set ID, returns content in XML or JSON format.

nodes.name: DailyMed_parse_adverse_reactions
nodes.description: Parse adverse reactions section from SPL XML into structured table format. Returns AE frequencies, severity, and descriptions. Example: setid='030d9bca-a934-6ef9-e063-6394a90a8277' extracts headache 10%, GI upset 5%.

drug_name.description: Drug name for automatic Set ID lookup (alternative to setid)

nodes.name: DailyMed_parse_dosing
nodes.description: Parse dosage and administration section from SPL XML. Returns structured dosing instructions, dose modifications, and administration guidelines. Example: standard dose, dose adjustments for renal/hepatic impairment.

drug_name.description: Drug name for automatic Set ID lookup (alternative to setid)

nodes.name: DailyMed_parse_contraindications
nodes.description: Parse contraindications section from SPL XML into structured list. Returns conditions, populations, or situations where drug should not be used. Example: pregnancy, hypersensitivity, severe renal impairment.

drug_name.description: Drug name for automatic Set ID lookup (alternative to setid)

nodes.name: DailyMed_parse_drug_interactions
nodes.description: Parse drug interactions section from SPL XML into structured format. Returns CYP substrates/inhibitors/inducers, P-gp interactions, and specific drug-drug interactions. Example: avoid with strong CYP3A4 inhibitors.

drug_name.description: Drug name for automatic Set ID lookup (alternative to setid)

nodes.name: DailyMed_parse_clinical_pharmacology
nodes.description: Parse clinical pharmacology section from SPL XML. Returns PK parameters (Cmax, Tmax, t1/2, AUC), PD effects, metabolism pathways, and elimination. Example: absorption, distribution, metabolism tables.

drug_name.description: Drug name for automatic Set ID lookup (alternative to setid)

nodes.name: FDA_OrangeBook_search_drug
nodes.description: Search FDA Drugs@FDA database by brand name, generic name, or application number. Returns approval status, products, and basic regulatory info. Example: brand_name='ADVIL' finds all Advil products with NDA numbers.

limit.description: Maximum number of results (1-100, default 10)

nodes.name: FDA_OrangeBook_get_approval_history
nodes.description: Get complete approval history for a drug application including original approval date, supplements, and review documents. Returns submission timeline and links to approval letters/labels. Example: NDA020402 shows all Advil approval actions.

nodes.name: FDA_OrangeBook_get_patent_info
nodes.description: Get patent information for approved drugs. Note: Full patent details (numbers, expiration dates) require Orange Book data files download. Returns guidance on accessing complete patent data. Use for regulatory exclusivity planning.

nodes.name: FDA_OrangeBook_get_exclusivity
nodes.description: Get drug exclusivity information (NCE, orphan, pediatric). Note: Specific expiration dates require Orange Book data files. Returns exclusivity types (5-year NCE, 3-year clinical study, 7-year orphan, 180-day generic). Use for generic launch planning.

nodes.name: FDA_OrangeBook_check_generic_availability
nodes.description: Check if generic versions are FDA-approved for a brand-name drug. Returns reference drug info, approved generics count, and TE codes. Example: Query 'LIPITOR' to find atorvastatin generics. Use for competitive landscape analysis.

brand_name.description: Brand name to check for generics

nodes.name: FDA_OrangeBook_get_te_code
nodes.description: Get therapeutic equivalence (TE) codes for drug products. AB=bioequivalent/substitutable, AT=topical equivalent, B*=NOT therapeutically equivalent. Returns TE codes with interpretation guide. Use for generic substitution decisions.

brand_name.description: Brand name to check TE codes

generic_name.description: Generic name to check TE codes

nodes.name: FDAGSRS_search_substances
nodes.description: Search FDA GSRS (Global Substance Registration System) for substances by name, UNII code, InChIKey, or formula. Returns official FDA substance records with UNII identifiers, substance class, structure, and cross-references (DrugBank, WHO-ATC, CAS, EC/EINECS, CFR). No API key required. Example: 'aspirin' -> UNII R16CO5Y76E, chemical, approved.

query.description: Search query: drug/chemical name, UNII code, InChIKey, or molecular formula. Examples: 'aspirin', 'semaglutide', 'R16CO5Y76E', 'C9H8O4', 'BSYNRYMUTXBXSQ-UHFFFAOYSA-N'.

substance_class.description: Filter by substance class. Options: 'chemical', 'protein', 'mixture', 'polymer', 'nucleicAcid', 'structurallyDiverse', 'specifiedSubstanceG1'. Default: no filter (all classes).

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-50, default 10).

nodes.name: FDAGSRS_get_substance
nodes.description: Get full FDA substance record by UNII (Unique Ingredient Identifier) code. Returns complete substance data including all names, cross-references (DrugBank, WHO-ATC, CAS, EC number, CFR), structure, and approval status. No API key required. UNII is the official FDA identifier for all regulated substances. Example: UNII 'R16CO5Y76E' = aspirin.

unii.description: FDA UNII (Unique Ingredient Identifier) code. 10-character alphanumeric string. Examples: 'R16CO5Y76E' (aspirin), '9100L32L2N' (ibuprofen), '4T6H12BN9U' (metformin), '57GNO57U7G' (semaglutide).

nodes.name: FDAGSRS_get_structure
nodes.description: Get chemical structure data for an FDA-regulated substance by UNII code. Returns SMILES, molecular formula, InChIKey, molfile, molecular weight, stereochemistry, and optical activity. No API key required. Only works for chemical substances; returns error for proteins, mixtures, or non-chemical substances. Example: UNII 'R16CO5Y76E' (aspirin) -> SMILES 'CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(O)=O'.

unii.description: FDA UNII code for the chemical substance. Examples: 'R16CO5Y76E' (aspirin), '9100L32L2N' (ibuprofen), '4T6H12BN9U' (metformin), 'IY9XDZ35W2' (cisplatin).

nodes.name: SEC_EDGAR_search_filings
nodes.description: Search SEC EDGAR full-text search index for company filings by keyword. Returns filing metadata including company name, CIK, form type, filing date, and accession number. Supports filtering by form type (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, S-1, etc.) and date range. No API key required. Use this to find SEC filings for public companies, discover CIK numbers, or track recent regulatory filings.

query.description: Search query for SEC filings (e.g., 'Moderna 10-K', 'CRISPR Therapeutics')

forms.description: Comma-separated form types to filter (e.g., '10-K', '10-K,8-K', '10-Q,S-1'). Common forms: 10-K (annual), 10-Q (quarterly), 8-K (current events), S-1 (IPO registration).

startdt.description: Start date filter in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2024-01-01')

enddt.description: End date filter in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2025-12-31')

nodes.name: SEC_EDGAR_get_company_submissions
nodes.description: Get company profile and recent filing history from SEC EDGAR by CIK number. Returns company name, tickers, exchanges, SIC code, state of incorporation, addresses, and up to 1000 recent filings with form types, dates, and accession numbers. No API key required. Use this after finding a CIK via SEC_EDGAR_search_filings to get a complete filing history.

cik.description: SEC Central Index Key, 10-digit zero-padded string (e.g., '0001682852' for Moderna). Find CIKs using SEC_EDGAR_search_filings.

nodes.name: FAERS_calculate_disproportionality
nodes.description: Calculate statistical disproportionality measures (ROR, PRR, IC) with 95% CI for drug-event pairs. Returns signal strength classification. Example: drug='ibuprofen', event='hepatotoxicity' → ROR=2.3 [1.8-2.9]. Use for pharmacovigilance signal detection.

adverse_event.description: MedDRA Preferred Term (e.g., 'Hepatotoxicity', 'Myopathy'). Use exact MedDRA Preferred Term capitalization (e.g., "Haemorrhage" not "hemorrhage").

reaction.description: Alias for adverse_event. MedDRA Preferred Term for the adverse drug reaction.

drug.description: Alias for drug_name. Generic drug name.

nodes.name: FAERS_stratify_by_demographics
nodes.description: Stratify adverse event reports by demographics (sex, age group, country). Returns counts and percentages by demographic group. Example: ibuprofen + GI bleeding stratified by sex → Female: 60%, Male: 40%. Use for subpopulation risk assessment.

adverse_event.description: MedDRA Preferred Term. Use exact MedDRA Preferred Term capitalization (e.g., "Haemorrhage" not "hemorrhage").

stratify_by.description: Demographic dimension to stratify by. Use "sex", "age", or "country" ("age_group" is also accepted as alias for "age").

reaction.description: Alias for adverse_event. MedDRA Preferred Term for the adverse drug reaction (e.g., "hemorrhage", "nausea").

demographic.description: Alias for stratify_by. Demographic dimension to stratify by (sex, age, or country).

drug.description: Alias for drug_name. Generic drug name.

nodes.name: FAERS_filter_serious_events
nodes.description: Filter for serious adverse events (death, hospitalization, disability, life-threatening). Returns top reactions meeting seriousness criteria. Example: fulvestrant serious events → Death: 150 cases, Hospitalization: 500 cases. Use for risk-benefit assessment.

seriousness_type.description: Type of serious event to filter

drug.description: Alias for drug_name. Generic drug name.

event_type.description: Alias for seriousness_type. Type of serious event (e.g., hospitalization, death, life_threatening).

adverse_event.description: Specific adverse event MedDRA term to filter within serious events (e.g., MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION, DEATH). Use uppercase MedDRA PT terms.

nodes.name: FAERS_compare_drugs
nodes.description: Compare safety signals for two drugs with the same adverse event. Returns ROR/PRR/IC for both drugs with comparative analysis. Example: compare fulvestrant vs tamoxifen for hepatotoxicity. Use for safety benchmarking.

adverse_event.description: MedDRA Preferred Term to compare. Use exact MedDRA Preferred Term capitalization (e.g., "Haemorrhage" not "hemorrhage").

reaction.description: Alias for adverse_event. MedDRA Preferred Term for the adverse drug reaction.

drugs.description: Alias for drug1/drug2. List of two drug names to compare, e.g., ["tofacitinib", "baricitinib"].

nodes.name: FAERS_analyze_temporal_trends
nodes.description: Analyze temporal trends in adverse event reporting by year. Returns yearly counts and trend direction (increasing/decreasing/stable). Example: ibuprofen GI bleeding reports 2015-2025 → +25% increase. Use for signal evolution monitoring.

adverse_event.description: MedDRA Preferred Term (optional, omit for all events). Use exact MedDRA Preferred Term capitalization (e.g., "Haemorrhage" not "hemorrhage").

reaction.description: Alias for adverse_event. MedDRA Preferred Term for the adverse drug reaction.

drug.description: Alias for drug_name. Generic drug name.

nodes.name: FAERS_rollup_meddra_hierarchy
nodes.description: Aggregate adverse events by MedDRA hierarchy (Preferred Term level). Returns top 50 PTs with counts. Note: Full HLT/SOC hierarchy requires MedDRA license. Use for adverse event profile overview.

drug.description: Alias for drug_name. Generic drug name.

nodes.name: cdc_data_search_datasets
nodes.description: Search for datasets on Data.CDC.gov (CDC's Socrata-based open data portal). Returns a list of available datasets matching search criteria. Use this to discover datasets before querying data.

limit.description: Maximum number of datasets to return (default: 50, max: 1000)

offset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination (default: 0)

nodes.name: cdc_data_get_dataset
nodes.description: Retrieve data from a specific CDC dataset on Data.CDC.gov. Requires a dataset ID (view ID) which can be found using cdc_data_search_datasets.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return (default: 100, max: 50000)

offset.description: Number of rows to skip for pagination (default: 0)

where_clause.description: Optional SoQL WHERE clause for filtering (e.g., "year = '2020'")

order_by.description: Optional column name to order by (e.g., 'year')

nodes.name: nhanes_get_dataset_info
nodes.description: Get information about NHANES (National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey) datasets. NHANES data is typically available as downloadable files (SAS, XPT formats) from the CDC website. This tool provides information about available datasets, years, and download links. Note: Actual data retrieval may require downloading and converting files.

component.description: Optional component type to filter datasets

nodes.name: nhanes_search_datasets
nodes.description: Search for NHANES datasets by keyword. Returns information about matching datasets including download links. Note: NHANES data files are typically in SAS/XPT format and may require conversion tools.

search_term.description: Search term to find datasets (e.g., 'glucose', 'blood pressure', 'diabetes')

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 20)

nodes.name: health_disparities_get_svi_info
nodes.description: Get information about CDC Social Vulnerability Index (SVI) data. SVI data is typically available as downloadable CSV files from the CDC ATSDR website. This tool provides information about available SVI datasets and download links. Note: Actual data retrieval may require downloading CSV files.

year.description: Optional year to filter SVI data (e.g., 2020, 2018, 2016). SVI is released every 2 years.

geography.description: Geographic level: 'county' for county-level, 'tract' for census tract, 'state' for state-level

nodes.name: health_disparities_get_county_rankings_info
nodes.description: Get information about County Health Rankings data. County Health Rankings provides county-level health data. This tool provides information about available datasets and access methods. Note: Data may be available as downloadable files or through their website.

year.description: Optional year for County Health Rankings data

state.description: Optional state abbreviation (e.g., 'CA', 'NY') to filter by state

nodes.name: HPA_generic_search
nodes.description: Generic search tool for Human Protein Atlas. Allows custom search queries and retrieval of specific columns.

columns.description: Comma-separated list of columns to retrieve. Defaults to 'g,gs,gd'. Common options: 'g' (Gene), 'gs' (Synonym), 'e' (Ensembl), 'u' (UniProt), 'pe' (Protein existence), 's' (Subcellular location), 'scml' (Main location), 'scal' (Addl location), 'rnat' (RNA tissue specificity), 'rnatsm' (RNA tissue nTPM), 'rnablm' (RNA blood nTPM), 'rnascm' (RNA single cell nTPM).

format.description: Response format (json or tsv). Defaults to 'json'.

nodes.name: HPA_search_genes_by_query
nodes.description: Search for matching genes by gene name, keywords, or cell line names and return Ensembl ID list. This is the entry tool for many HPA query workflows.

search_query.description: Gene name, alias, keyword, or cell line name to search for, e.g., 'EGFR', 'TP53', or 'MCF7'.

nodes.name: HPA_get_comparative_expression_by_gene_and_cellline
nodes.description: Compare the expression level differences of a gene between a specific cell line and healthy tissues using gene name and cell line name.

gene_name.description: Gene name or gene symbol, e.g., 'TP53', 'BRCA1', 'EGFR', etc.

cell_line.description: Cell line name, supported cell lines include: ishikawa, hela, mcf7, a549, hepg2, jurkat, pc3, rh30, siha, u251.

nodes.name: HPA_get_disease_expression_by_gene_tissue_disease
nodes.description: Compare the expression level of a gene in specific disease state versus healthy state using gene name, tissue type, and disease name.

gene_name.description: Gene name or gene symbol, e.g., 'TP53', 'BRCA1', 'KRAS', etc.

tissue_type.description: Tissue type, e.g., 'brain', 'breast', 'colon', 'lung', etc., optional parameter.

disease_name.description: Disease name, supported diseases include: brain_cancer, breast_cancer, colon_cancer, lung_cancer, liver_cancer, prostate_cancer, kidney_cancer, pancreatic_cancer, stomach_cancer, ovarian_cancer.

nodes.name: HPA_get_biological_processes_by_gene
nodes.description: Get biological process information for a gene, with special focus on key processes like apoptosis, cell cycle, etc.

gene_name.description: Gene name or gene symbol, e.g., 'TP53', 'CDK1', 'CASP3', etc.

filter_processes.description: Whether to filter and focus on specific biological processes (apoptosis, cell cycle, transcription regulation, etc.), defaults to true.

nodes.name: HPA_get_comprehensive_gene_details_by_ensembl_id
nodes.description: Get detailed in-depth information from gene page using Ensembl Gene ID, including image URLs, antibody data, protein expression, and comprehensive information. This is the core tool for retrieving all images (tissue immunohistochemistry, subcellular immunofluorescence).

ensembl_id.description: Ensembl Gene ID, e.g., 'ENSG00000064787' (BCAS1), 'ENSG00000141510' (TP53), etc. Usually obtained through HPA_search_genes_by_query tool.

include_images.description: Whether to include image URL information (immunofluorescence, cell line images, etc.), defaults to true.

include_antibodies.description: Whether to include detailed antibody information (validation status, Western blot data, etc.), defaults to true.

include_expression.description: Whether to include detailed expression data (tissue specificity, subcellular localization, etc.), defaults to true.

nodes.name: HPA_get_cancer_prognostics_by_gene
nodes.description: Retrieve prognostic value of a gene across various cancer types, indicating if its expression level correlates with patient survival outcomes.

ensembl_id.description: Ensembl Gene ID of the gene to check, e.g., 'ENSG00000141510' for TP53, 'ENSG00000012048' for BRCA1.

nodes.name: HPA_get_protein_interactions_by_gene
nodes.description: Fetch known protein-protein interaction partners for a given gene from Human Protein Atlas database.

gene_name.description: Official gene symbol, e.g., 'EGFR', 'TP53', 'BRCA1', etc.

nodes.name: HPA_get_rna_expression_in_specific_tissues
nodes.description: Query RNA expression levels (nTPM) for a specific gene in one or more user-specified tissues with precise tissue matching.

ensembl_id.description: Ensembl Gene ID for the gene, e.g., 'ENSG00000141510' for TP53.

tissue_names.description: List of tissue names to query, e.g., ['brain', 'liver', 'heart muscle', 'kidney']. Case-insensitive matching is supported.

nodes.name: HPA_get_contextual_biological_process_analysis
nodes.description: Analyze a gene's biological processes in the context of a specific tissue or cell line by integrating functional annotation with expression data to determine functional relevance.

gene_name.description: Gene name or symbol, e.g., 'TP53', 'EGFR', 'BRCA1'.

context_name.description: Name of the tissue or cell line to provide context, e.g., 'brain', 'liver', 'hela', 'mcf7'.

nodes.name: HPA_get_rna_expression_by_source
nodes.description: Get RNA expression level (nTPM) for a gene in a specific biological source using optimized columns parameter. Supports tissue, blood, brain, and single_cell source types with comprehensive source mappings.

gene_name.description: Gene name or gene symbol, e.g., 'GFAP', 'TP53', 'BRCA1', etc.

source_type.description: The type of biological source. Choose from: 'tissue', 'blood', 'brain', 'single_cell'.

source_name.description: The specific name of the biological source, e.g., 'liver', 'heart_muscle', 't_cell', 'hepatocytes', 'cerebellum'. Must be a valid name from the comprehensive HPA columns mapping.

nodes.name: HPA_get_subcellular_location
nodes.description: Get annotated subcellular locations for a protein using optimized columns parameter. Retrieves both main and additional subcellular locations efficiently.

gene_name.description: Gene name or gene symbol, e.g., 'CCNB1', 'TP53', 'EGFR', etc.

nodes.name: HPA_get_gene_basic_info_by_ensembl_id
nodes.description: Get gene basic information and expression data from Human Protein Atlas using Ensembl Gene ID. Enhanced version now uses efficient JSON API.

nodes.name: HPA_get_gene_tsv_data_by_ensembl_id
nodes.description: Get detailed gene data in TSV format from Human Protein Atlas using Ensembl Gene ID (backward compatibility tool).

nodes.name: Reactome_get_pathway_reactions
nodes.description: Query all reactions and subpathways contained under a Pathway using Pathway Stable ID. Returns list of events (reactions and subpathways) contained in the pathway, including their metadata and relationships.

stId.description: Pathway Stable ID (e.g., 'R-HSA-73817'). To find pathway IDs, use Reactome_list_top_pathways to get top-level pathways, or Reactome_map_uniprot_to_pathways if you have a UniProt protein ID.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_pathway
nodes.description: Get detailed information about a specific pathway by its Stable ID. Returns comprehensive pathway data including metadata, compartments, literature references, orthologous events, and related pathways. Uses the unified query endpoint that works for all entity types.

stId.description: Pathway Stable ID (e.g., 'R-HSA-73817'). To find pathway IDs, use Reactome_list_top_pathways to get top-level pathways, or Reactome_map_uniprot_to_pathways if you have a UniProt protein ID.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_pathway_hierarchy
nodes.description: Get the hierarchy (parent pathways) for a pathway. Returns list of parent pathways.

stId.description: Pathway Stable ID (e.g., 'R-HSA-73817'). To find pathway IDs, use Reactome_list_top_pathways to get top-level pathways, or Reactome_map_uniprot_to_pathways if you have a UniProt protein ID.

nodes.name: Reactome_list_top_pathways
nodes.description: List top-level pathways for a specific species. Returns pathways that have no parent pathways, including their metadata and basic information.

species.description: Species name or taxonomy ID (e.g., 'Homo sapiens' or '9606'). IMPORTANT: Common names like 'human' or 'mouse' will return HTTP 404. Use exact scientific name (e.g., 'Homo sapiens', 'Mus musculus') or NCBI taxonomy ID. To find available species, use Reactome_list_species.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_reaction
nodes.description: Get detailed information about a specific reaction/event by its Stable ID. Returns comprehensive reaction data including inputs, outputs, regulation, literature references, and participants. Uses the unified query endpoint. Use Reactome_get_pathway_reactions to get valid reaction IDs first.

stId.description: Reaction Stable ID (e.g., 'R-HSA-111289'). To find reaction IDs, use Reactome_get_pathway_reactions with a pathway ID to get all reactions in that pathway.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_complex
nodes.description: Get detailed information about a protein complex by its Stable ID. Returns comprehensive complex data including subunits, compartments, and related information. Uses the unified query endpoint that works for all entity types.

stId.description: Complex Stable ID (e.g., 'R-HSA-XXXXX'). To find complex IDs, use Reactome_get_participants with a reaction ID to see complexes participating in that reaction.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_interactor
nodes.description: Get detailed information about an interactor (protein, complex, or small molecule) by its Stable ID. Returns comprehensive interactor data including reference entities, compartments, and related pathways. Uses the unified query endpoint that works for all entity types.

stId.description: Interactor Stable ID. To find interactor IDs, use Reactome_get_participants with a reaction ID, or Reactome_map_uniprot_to_pathways/Reactome_map_uniprot_to_reactions if you have a UniProt protein ID.

nodes.name: Reactome_list_species
nodes.description: List all species available in Reactome database.

nodes.name: Reactome_query_by_ids
nodes.description: Query Reactome by providing a list of Reactome stable identifiers (R-HSA-*). Returns detailed information about pathways, reactions, or other entities. Note: This endpoint requires Reactome stable IDs, not external IDs (UniProt, ChEBI). Use mapping endpoints first to convert external IDs to Reactome IDs.

ids.description: List of Reactome stable identifiers (e.g., 'R-HSA-73817', 'R-HSA-111289'). Must be Reactome stable IDs, not external IDs. To find pathway IDs, use Reactome_list_top_pathways. To find reaction IDs, use Reactome_get_pathway_reactions. To convert external IDs (UniProt) to Reactome IDs, first use Reactome_map_uniprot_to_pathways or Reactome_map_uniprot_to_reactions.

species.description: Optional: Filter by species using exact scientific name (e.g., 'Homo sapiens') or taxonomy ID. Common names like 'human' are not supported.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_database_version
nodes.description: Get Reactome database version information. Returns version number as integer.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_event_ancestors
nodes.description: Get ancestor events (parent pathways) for a reaction or pathway.

stId.description: Event Stable ID (pathway or reaction, e.g., 'R-HSA-73817'). To find pathway IDs, use Reactome_list_top_pathways. To find reaction IDs, use Reactome_get_pathway_reactions with a pathway ID.

nodes.name: Reactome_map_uniprot_to_pathways
nodes.description: Map a UniProt protein identifier to Reactome pathways. Returns all pathways that contain this protein.

uniprot_id.description: UniProt protein accession (e.g., 'P04637' for TP53, 'P00533' for EGFR)

nodes.name: Reactome_map_uniprot_to_reactions
nodes.description: Map a UniProt protein identifier to Reactome reactions. Returns all reactions that involve this protein.

uniprot_id.description: UniProt protein accession (e.g., 'P04637' for TP53, 'P00533' for EGFR)

nodes.name: Reactome_get_participants
nodes.description: Get all participants (physical entities) of a reaction or event. Returns list of physical entities (proteins, complexes, small molecules) participating in the event, including their reference entities.

stId.description: Event Stable ID (reaction or pathway, e.g., 'R-HSA-111289'). To find reaction IDs, use Reactome_get_pathway_reactions with a pathway ID. To find pathway IDs, use Reactome_list_top_pathways.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_participant_reference_entities
nodes.description: Get reference entities for participants of a reaction or event. Returns list of reference entities (UniProt proteins, ChEBI chemicals, etc.) associated with the participants, including database identifiers and names.

stId.description: Event Stable ID (reaction or pathway, e.g., 'R-HSA-111289'). To find reaction IDs, use Reactome_get_pathway_reactions with a pathway ID. To find pathway IDs, use Reactome_list_top_pathways.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_pathways_low_entity
nodes.description: Get low-level pathways (most specific pathways) containing a specific entity. Returns pathways at the lowest level of the hierarchy.

id.description: Entity Stable ID (pathway, reaction, or physical entity, e.g., 'R-HSA-73817'). To find pathway IDs, use Reactome_list_top_pathways. To find reaction IDs, use Reactome_get_pathway_reactions. To find entity IDs, use Reactome_get_participants.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_entity_events
nodes.description: Get events (reactions/subpathways) associated with an entity. Returns TSV-formatted event data parsed into structured JSON. The API returns tab-separated values (ID, name, type) which are automatically converted to JSON format.

stId.description: Entity Stable ID (pathway, e.g., 'R-HSA-73817'). To find pathway IDs, use Reactome_list_top_pathways to get top-level pathways, or Reactome_map_uniprot_to_pathways if you have a UniProt protein ID.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_entity_compartment
nodes.description: Get compartment information for an entity. Returns TSV-formatted compartment data parsed into structured JSON. The API returns tab-separated values (ID, name, type) which are automatically converted to JSON format.

stId.description: Entity Stable ID (e.g., 'R-HSA-73817'). To find pathway IDs, use Reactome_list_top_pathways. To find reaction IDs, use Reactome_get_pathway_reactions. To find entity IDs, use Reactome_get_participants.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_events_hierarchy
nodes.description: Get the full event hierarchy (pathways and reactions) for a specific species. Returns complete nested hierarchy structure with top-level pathways and their children (subpathways and reactions). Note: Use taxonomy ID (e.g., '9606') for best results.

species.description: Species taxonomy ID (e.g., '9606' for Homo sapiens) or species name. Taxonomy ID is recommended. To find available species and their taxonomy IDs, use Reactome_list_species.

nodes.name: Reactome_get_diseases
nodes.description: Get list of disease pathways or DOIDs (Disease Ontology IDs) annotated in Reactome. Returns disease-related pathway information including database identifiers, definitions, and names.

nodes.name: PubChem_get_associated_patents_by_CID
nodes.description: Get a list of patents associated with a specific compound CID.

cid.description: PubChem compound ID to query, e.g., 2244 (Aspirin).

nodes.name: PubChem_get_compound_properties_by_CID
nodes.description: Get a set of specified molecular properties through CID (Compound ID), such as molecular weight, IUPAC name, Canonical SMILES.

cid.description: PubChem compound ID to query, e.g., 2244 (Aspirin).

nodes.name: PubChem_get_CID_by_compound_name
nodes.description: Search PubChem by compound name to get CID(s). This is the PRIMARY tool for looking up a drug or chemical by name (e.g., 'cytarabine', 'aspirin', 'metformin', 'venetoclax') to obtain PubChem Compound IDs. Use the returned CID with other PubChem tools for properties, patents, etc. Only works with chemical/drug names, not disease names.

name.description: Chemical compound name (e.g., "Aspirin", "Acetaminophen") or IUPAC name. Do not use disease names or medical conditions.

compound_name.description: Alias for name. The compound name to look up.

nodes.name: PubChem_get_CID_by_SMILES
nodes.description: Retrieve corresponding CID list by SMILES string.

smiles.description: SMILES expression (e.g., "CC(=O)OC1=CC=CC=C1C(=O)O" corresponds to aspirin).

nodes.name: PubChem_search_compounds_by_substructure
nodes.description: Search for CIDs in PubChem that contain the given substructure (SMILES). Returns up to max_results CIDs (default 10).

smiles.description: SMILES of substructure (e.g., "c1ccccc1" corresponds to benzene ring).

max_results.description: Maximum number of CIDs to return (default: 10, max: 10000).

nodes.name: PubChem_search_compounds_by_similarity
nodes.description: Search by similarity (Tanimoto coefficient), returns CID list of compounds with similarity above threshold to given SMILES molecule. Returns up to max_results CIDs (default 10).

smiles.description: SMILES expression of target molecule.

threshold.description: Similarity threshold (between 0 and 1), e.g., 0.9 means 90% similarity.

max_results.description: Maximum number of CIDs to return (default: 10, max: 10000).

nodes.name: PubChem_get_compound_2D_image_by_CID
nodes.description: Get 2D structure image (PNG format) of compound by CID.

cid.description: Compound ID to get image for, e.g., 2244.

nodes.name: PubChem_get_compound_synonyms_by_CID
nodes.description: Get complete list of synonyms for compound by CID.

cid.description: Compound ID to query synonyms for, e.g., 2244.

nodes.name: PubChem_get_compound_xrefs_by_CID
nodes.description: Get external references (XRefs) for compound by CID, including links to ChEBI, DrugBank, KEGG, etc.

cid.description: Compound ID to query external references for, e.g., 2244.

xref_types.description: List of external database types to query, e.g., ["RegistryID", "RN", "PubMedID"].

nodes.name: PubChem_get_assay_description
nodes.description: Get bioassay description and metadata by AID. Returns assay name, source, protocol, target information, activity outcomes, and data columns. Essential for understanding what an assay measures before retrieving results.

nodes.name: PubChem_get_assay_summary
nodes.description: Get summary statistics for a bioassay by AID. Returns counts of active/inactive compounds, target information, and assay metadata. Useful for quick assessment of assay size and hit rate.

nodes.name: PubChem_get_assay_targets
nodes.description: Get protein/gene targets for a bioassay. Returns GI numbers, protein names, gene IDs, and gene symbols. Critical for understanding what molecular targets an assay screens against.

nodes.name: PubChem_search_assays_by_target_gene
nodes.description: Search bioassays by target gene symbol. Returns AIDs of assays that screen against the specified gene. Example: USP2 returns assays targeting USP2 protease.

gene_symbol.description: Gene symbol to search (e.g., EGFR, USP2, TP53)

nodes.name: PubChem_search_assays_by_type
nodes.description: Search bioassays by assay type. Types: screening (HTS), confirmatory, doseresponse, summary, cellbased, biochemical, invivo, invitro. Returns list of matching AIDs.

assay_type.description: Type of assay to search for

nodes.name: PubChem_get_compound_bioactivity
nodes.description: Get bioassay results summary for a compound by CID. Returns all assays the compound was tested in, activity outcomes (active/inactive), and target information. Critical for understanding compound's biological profile.

nodes.name: PubChem_get_assay_active_compounds
nodes.description: Get CIDs of compounds that are ACTIVE in a bioassay. Returns compound IDs that passed the activity threshold. Use for hit identification from HTS screens.

nodes.name: PubChem_get_assay_dose_response
nodes.description: Get dose-response data from a bioassay. Returns concentration-response curves for tested compounds. Essential for determining IC50/EC50 values. Limited to dose-response type assays.

aid.description: PubChem BioAssay ID (must be a dose-response assay)

nodes.name: PubChem_get_assays_for_compound_active
